book_title: The New Market Wizards: Conversations with America's Top Traders
author: Jack D. Schwager

traders.name: Bill Lipschutz
key_rules: Scale into and out of positions - never all-in or all-out at once
Don't need to be right >50% of the time - ride winners, cut losers
Missing an opportunity is as bad as being on the wrong side
When hurt in the market, get out immediately regardless of price
Reduce position size during losing streaks to restore confidence
Always know exactly where you stand - risk control is paramount
Don't concentrate too much capital in one trade or correlated group
If fundamentals change but price doesn't confirm, get out

traders.name: Randy McKay
key_rules: Watch how markets respond to fundamental information, not the info itself
If bad news can't push market down, it's going higher (and vice versa)
Never try to pick tops or bottoms - trade the middle of the move
When government intervention fails to hold a level, trade against it
When hurt, get out immediately - decisions impaired after losses
Prognostication 25%, execution 75% in modern markets
Trade easy part of moves - beginning and end are hardest

traders.name: William Eckhardt
key_rules: Don't buy retracements - buy strength (if market retraced enough to matter, trade isn't as good)
Avoid comfort - comfortable trades are usually wrong
RSI, stochastics, Fibonacci, Gann are near worthless
Moving averages work if you add risk management
Missing a good trade is worse than taking a bad trade
System should have 3-4 parameters max (7-8 is too many)
Use robust statistics, not delicate ones - weight indicators 1 or 0
If system results don't sock you in the eye, not worth pursuing
Try your best to disprove your system - if it survives, maybe it's valid
Chart patterns: 98% of what looks good doesn't work
Mechanical system outperformed his discretionary overrides
Overriding should be rare - save ingenuity for research, not trading

traders.name: Monroe Trout
key_rules: Risk management rules: 1.5% max loss per trade, 4% max loss per day, 10% max loss per month
Set maximum position size per market at start of each month
If down 4% in a day, close ALL positions and wait until next day
Best daily Sharpe ratio = best trader (not just return)
Moving averages are useful indicators; Fibonacci, Gann, RSI, stochastics are not
Diversify across strategies AND time horizons
50% system, 50% discretionary execution timing
Stops: don't place at obvious levels (above/below prior day high/low)
Instead, place 10 ticks before or 10 ticks after the obvious level
Markets drawn to round numbers (magnet effect) - trade into them
Play for streaks: trade bigger when winning, smaller when losing
67% avg annual return with max 8% drawdown over 5 years

traders.name: Al Weiss
key_rules: Study 100+ years of chart history for patterns
Factor economic cycle position (inflationary vs deflationary) into pattern interpretation
Look for patterns within patterns within patterns (nested confirmation)
A 50% accurate pattern is profitable with good risk control
Follow system >90% of the time, override only selectively
52% average annual return, max 17% drawdown over 10 years
Total risk on all open positions never exceeds 4%

traders.name: Stanley Druckenmiller
key_rules: Focus on liquidity and central bank policy, not earnings
When you have conviction, bet big (Soros influence: 'It's not whether you're right or wrong, but how much you make when right')
Cut losses quickly - never let a position become a prayer
Watch what market does on news more than the news itself
If market can't rally on bullish news, it's going lower

traders.name: Richard Driehaus
key_rules: Buy high, sell higher - don't buy low
Stock must have accelerating earnings AND revenue growth
Relative strength must be top 10-20% of market
Use charts for timing but fundamentals for selection
Buy on big gap-up days on significant news (don't wait for pullback)
More losers than winners is fine - cut losses, ride winners
Small cap, lightly covered stocks offer greatest inefficiency
P/E ratios mostly irrelevant for growth stocks
Never buy a stock that's dropping, even with good fundamentals
A few home runs account for bulk of performance

traders.name: Gil Blake
key_rules: 5 steps: (1) find vehicle that suits personality (2) identify non-random behavior (3) prove it statistically (4) set trading rules (5) follow rules
Markets are mostly random but have pockets of non-randomness
45% avg annual return, 96% winning months, worst year +24%
Holding period 1-4 days, purely technical models
Adaptability is key: when approach stops working, research new one
Do your own thing (independence) + do the right thing (discipline)

traders.name: Victor Sperandeo
key_rules: Markets have statistically significant life-expectancy profiles
Median intermediate bull market upmove is 20% over 107 days
Once move exceeds median age, technical signals become MORE reliable
Don't apply technical patterns indiscriminately - context of market age matters
Always predetermine exit point before entering trade
When trading large, positions must make money immediately or cut
Stay in business first, get rich second
18 consecutive winning years, 72% avg annual return
Poker analogy: speculating (odds in favor) vs gambling (odds against)

traders.name: Tom Basso
key_rules: Normalize volatility across all positions (equal volatility weighting)
Use trailing stops based on ATR
Stress-free trading through complete systematic approach
Focus on process not outcome
If system has edge, let it play out over hundreds of trades

traders.name: Linda Bradford Raschke
key_rules: Trade pattern recognition: 3-pushes to a high/low, Wolfe Waves
Look for divergence between price and oscillators at extremes
First hour range breakout as day-trade signal
Swing trading: 2-5 day holding period
Cut losses ruthlessly - if trade doesn't work in expected time, exit
Buy strength, sell weakness - same as Eckhardt's philosophy
Market internal indicators more useful than price indicators alone

traders.name: Mark Ritchie
key_rules: Patience - wait for the perfect trade
When convinced, bet big
Cut losses immediately when wrong

traders.name: Joe Ritchie
key_rules: Theoretical edge from options mispricing
Risk management: know your exposure at all times
Simplify complex problems to their essence

traders.name: Blair Hull
key_rules: Find an edge (statistical), then manage risk
Blackjack card-counting analogy: bet more when odds favor you
Options: buy underpriced volatility, sell overpriced volatility
Discipline is more important than intelligence

traders.name: Jeff Yass
key_rules: Think in terms of expected value, not win rate
Diversify across many small-edge trades
Don't get emotionally attached to any single position
Horse racing analogy: bet when odds are mispriced

traders.name: Charles Faulkner
key_rules: Model the strategies of successful traders using NLP
Beliefs drive behavior drive results
Internal representations determine trading decisions
Reframe losses as cost of doing business

traders.name: Robert Krausz
key_rules: Subconscious mind drives trading decisions more than conscious
Visualization and mental rehearsal improve execution
Identify and remove self-sabotaging beliefs

strategies.name: Eckhardt Robust Trend System
strategies.entry_logic: Buy breakouts on strength (NOT retracements). Enter when price exceeds recent range with momentum. Mechanical system with 3-4 parameters max.
strategies.exit_logic: Trailing stop. Override only when system clearly violates design intent (e.g., staying short bonds during a stock crash).
strategies.position_sizing: Equal risk across all trades. Either take a trade at full size or don't take it at all (1 or 0 weighting).
strategies.take_profit: Ride the trend - no fixed targets
strategies.notes: Co-creator of Turtle Trading. Proved mechanical system beat his own discretionary overrides. Key insight: avoid comfort, buy strength not weakness.

strategies.name: Trout Statistical Multi-System
strategies.entry_logic: Dozens of statistical models running simultaneously. 50% system signals, 50% discretionary timing. Uses magnet effect (buy ahead of round numbers). Monitors floor noise and order flow for confirmation.
strategies.exit_logic: Hard rules: exit if down 1.5% on single trade, exit ALL if down 4% on day, exit ALL if down 10% in month. Mental stops, not placed stops.
strategies.position_sizing: Max position per market set monthly. Trade bigger during winning streaks, smaller during losing.
strategies.stop_loss: Mental stops - avoid obvious placement (not 1 tick above/below prior high/low)
filters: Avoid placing stops at obvious levels
strategies.notes: 67% avg annual return, max 8% drawdown. Best return/risk ratio of 100+ CTAs surveyed. Low transaction costs are critical edge.

strategies.name: Driehaus Growth Momentum
strategies.entry_logic: Buy stocks with accelerating earnings AND revenue growth. Relative strength top 10-20%. Buy on big gap-up days on significant news. Never buy declining stocks. Buy near highs, not near lows.
strategies.exit_logic: Sell when: earnings decelerate, technical breakdown, fundamental story changes. Cut losses but no fixed stop - balance conviction with price action.
strategies.position_sizing: Largest positions on highest-conviction ideas (concentrated portfolio). More losers than winners is acceptable.
strategies.stop_loss: No fixed stop - fundamental + technical deterioration triggers exit
strategies.take_profit: Hold as long as fundamental and technical conditions intact. Don't cut winners short.
filters: Market environment must favor growth stocks
strategies.notes: Very similar to O'Neil/CANSLIM but more aggressive on entry timing. Buys on strength, not pullbacks. Key: few home runs account for bulk of returns.

strategies.name: Sperandeo Market Age Analysis
strategies.entry_logic: Classify market age using historical duration/magnitude statistics. Median bull intermediate move = 20% over 107 days. When move exceeds median, apply technical analysis with higher confidence. Look for reversal patterns ONLY in 'old age' markets.
strategies.exit_logic: Predetermined exit point set BEFORE entry. When trading large, exit immediately if not profitable quickly.
strategies.position_sizing: Reduce to 50% max when market exceeds historical median move. Scale based on market age (young = bigger, old = smaller).
filters: Market must be classified by bull/bear cycle age
Don't apply reversal patterns to 'young' markets
Dow Theory confirmation for major turns
strategies.notes: 18 consecutive winning years, 72% avg annual return. Unique approach of combining market life-expectancy with technical analysis. Very relevant to systematic trading.

strategies.name: Basso Volatility-Normalized Trend
strategies.entry_logic: Trend-following signals with volatility normalization. All positions sized to equal volatility contribution.
strategies.exit_logic: ATR-based trailing stops. Systematic - no discretionary overrides.
strategies.notes: Mr. Serenity - completely stress-free systematic approach. Focus on process. Very similar to SBRS risk management principles.

strategies.name: Raschke Swing Trading Patterns
strategies.entry_logic: Pattern recognition: 3-pushes to high/low (reversal signal), first-hour range breakout (day trade), Wolfe Waves. Confirm with oscillator divergence at extremes.
strategies.exit_logic: Time stop: if trade doesn't work in expected time, exit. Cut losses ruthlessly.
strategies.notes: One of few female Market Wizards. Swing trading approach with 2-5 day holding period. Very pattern-focused but disciplined.

strategies.name: Blake Mutual Fund Timing
strategies.entry_logic: Purely technical models identifying non-random price behavior in mutual fund NAVs. Switch between sector funds and money market based on signals. 1-4 day holding period.
filters: Must identify statistically significant non-random behavior
strategies.notes: 45% avg annual, 96% winning months, worst year +24%. Unique niche: mutual fund switching. Not directly applicable to futures/forex but demonstrates that statistical edges exist.

strategies.name: McKay News Reaction System
strategies.entry_logic: Watch how market responds to fundamental information. If bad news fails to push market down, buy. If good news fails to push market up, sell. Enter after confirmation of non-reaction.
strategies.exit_logic: When move decelerates or contrary news reaction observed. Exit immediately when hurt.
strategies.position_sizing: Scale with market ease - bigger in trending markets, smaller in choppy
strategies.stop_loss: Exit immediately when hurt, don't wait
strategies.take_profit: Ride the middle of the move, don't try to pick tops/bottoms
filters: Government intervention failing = trade against it
Only trade easy part of moves
strategies.notes: $2K to tens of millions over 20 years, 18/20 profitable years. News reaction concept is codifiable but requires judgment on what constitutes 'significant' news.

cross_cutting_themes.consensus: Predetermine risk before entry. Hard rules: Trout's 1.5%/4%/10% limits. Sperandeo's predetermined exit. Universal agreement: survive first, profit second.
Eckhardt proved his system outperformed him. Save creativity for research, not trading. Override only in unprecedented situations.
Don't buy retracements. If market retraced enough to matter, the trade isn't as good. Buy high, sell higher.
Pockets of non-random behavior exist and are tradeable. But most apparent patterns are noise. Statistically prove your edge.
RSI, stochastics, Fibonacci retracements, Gann angles show near-zero edge. Moving averages are the one public indicator worth using.
How the market responds to news matters more than the news. If market can't rally on bullish news, it's going lower.
